GS upgrades Societe Generale to 'buy' on efficient cost control
data.description:
** Goldman Sachs upgrades French multinational bank Societe Generale to "buy" from "neutral", saying the bank is improving cost control which should lead to higher profits

** The brokerage forecasts a sequential improvement in the bank's cost-to-income ratio, a key measure of profitability, dropping from 60% in 2026 to 57% by 2028

** This "efficiency-led plan" is expected to boost earnings per share by around 19% between 2026 and 2028, above the sector average of around 11%

** GS expects the bank to deliver double-digit profitability from 2026 onwards, while its valuation remains low relative to peers despite a strong 2025 performance

** Based on this outlook, Goldman Sachs raised its price target for the stock by about 16% to 72.25 euros

** Out of 22 analysts that cover Societe Generale SA, 18 rate the stock "strong buy" or "buy,"​three rate "hold" and one rates the stock "sell" - LSEG data

data.title: Marks Electrical: Millard to Step Dn Dec 31
UK's Frasers says market conditions tough but keeps profit outlook
data.description:
Frasers , the British sportswear and fashion retailer that is majority owned by Mike Ashley, reaffirmed its full-year profit forecast despite what it called tough market conditions.

Profit for the six months to October 26 fell by 2.8% to 291 million pounds ($388 million) said Frasers, which owns Flannels and Sports Direct, and the group added that while it was cautious about the second-half, it was on track.

"We've made a solid start to full-year 2026 even though market conditions are tough, consumer confidence is very subdued and excess inventory continues to weigh on the industry, leading to increased promotional activity," CEO Michael Murray said on Thursday.

Official data published last month showed British retail sales tumbled in October, while a closely watched gauge of household sentiment fell this month, adding to signs of waning consumer spending ahead of last week's tax raising government budget.

With uncertainty regarding the budget now behind them, retailers are hoping that consumer confidence rebounds in the Christmas trading period.

For the full-year, Frasers, which has 1,500 UK stores, expects adjusted pretax profit of 550 million pounds to 600 million pounds, which compares with the 560 million pounds it made last year, helped by plans to offset higher costs with efficiencies and synergies.

($1 = 0.7500 pounds)

data.title: Japan futures slip on soft demand
data.description:
• Japanese rubber futures fell on Thursday, as weak supply-demand fundamentals overshadowed support from firmer oil.

  • The Osaka Exchange (OSE) rubber contract for May delivery , was down 1.4 yen, or 0.43%, at 324 yen($2.09) per kg.

  • The rubber contract on the Shanghai Futures Exchange (SHFE) for May delivery dipped 190 yuan, or 1.25%, to 15,040 yuan ($2,128.05) per metric ton.

  • The most active January butadiene rubber contract on the SHFE (SHBRv1) fell 210 yuan, or 1.97%, to 10,455 yuan per metric ton.

  • Rubber fundamentals are weak in both supply and demand, and prices are expected to be volatile, said Chinese broker Everbright Futures.

  • Global rubber consumption is expected to decrease by 1.8% in the first 10 months of 2025, leading to oversupply in the spot market, increased inventory pressure, and significant downward pressure on prices, said Chinese commodity information provider SunSirs.

  • The yen dipped to 155.47 per dollar, but has inched away from the 10-month low it hit last month.

  • A stronger currency makes yen-denominated assets less affordable to overseas buyers.

  • Japan's Nikkei climbed by 2.2%.

  • Oil prices firmed on potential supply constraints following Ukrainian attacks on Russia's oil infrastructure.

  • Natural rubber often takes direction from oil prices as it competes for a market share with synthetic rubber, which is made from crude oil.

  • Top rubber producer Thailand's meteorological agency warned of thundershowers and accumulated rains from December 4-6.

  • Broadly, top rubber consumer China is likely to stick to its current annual economic growth target of around 5% next year, with the immediate focus on fiscal and monetary support amid weak consumer demand.

  • The front-month rubber contract on Singapore Exchange's SICOM platform for December delivery last traded at 170.1 U.S. cents per kg, down 0.7%.

($1 = 155.3100 yen)

($1 = 7.0675 Chinese yuan)

data.title: VOD: US$2.1B acquisition of 20% stake consolidates control and accelerates regional fintech growth
data.description:
A 20% stake in a leading East African telecom and fintech operator is being acquired for US$2.1 billion, consolidating control and supporting regional growth and financial inclusion. The deal is expected to close in Q1 2026, pending regulatory approvals.

Original document: Vodacom Group Limited [VOD] Press release — Dec. 4 2025

EU to Probe Meta Over WhatsApp's AI Features, the FT Says, Citing Sources
data.description:
• The European Union's antitrust authorities are set to launch an investigation into social-media giant Meta Platforms relating to the use of artificial-intelligence features on its messaging service WhatsApp, the Financial Times reports, citing unnamed sources.
  • The probe by the European Commission, the EU's executive arm, could be announced over the coming days, the FT reports.
  • Spokespeople for the commission declined to respond to the FT. Meta declined to comment on the probe.

Full story: https://shorturl.at/mFzw4

Write to Joshua Kirby at joshua.kirby@wsj.com; @joshualeokirby

data.title: Fitch Affirms Northern Star Resources at 'BBB-'; Outlook Stable
data.description:
Fitch Ratings-Sydney-04 December 2025:

Fitch Ratings has affirmed Australia-based Northern Star Resources Ltd's (NST) Long-Term Foreign-Currency Issuer Default Rating (IDR) at 'BBB-'. The Outlook is Stable. Fitch has also affirmed NST's US dollar senior unsecured notes at 'BBB-'.

NST's rating is underpinned by its position as a well-established gold producer with a diverse portfolio of mines across the Tier 1 mining jurisdictions of Australia and the US, large operational scale, and ore reserves of 22.3 million ounces (oz), which support healthy mine life of 14 years at the production rate of the financial year ended June 2025 (FY25). The company is progressing through mine developments that will support group production at above 2 million oz of gold and improve its cost position into the first half of the industry cost curve to better align with its higher-rated peers.

The Stable Outlook reflects NST's strong financial profile, which is supported by its robust liquidity, operational flexibility and low leverage. We expect NST's EBITDA leverage to remain low despite increasing growth capex for its Kalgoorlie Consolidated Gold Mines (KCGM) mill expansion project.

Key Rating Drivers

Cost Position to Improve: We expect the completion of the KCGM mill expansion to ease rising pressure on NST's all-in sustaining cost (AISC), which has been driven by industry-wide inflation and extensive mine developments. The expanded mill capacity of 27 million tonnes per annum (mtpa) by 2029 will enable utilisation of 2.9 million oz contained in ore stockpiles at the Kalgoorlie production centre with no associated mining costs.

In addition, the company is progressing with optimising the output of its core mines and cost improvements. This should support management's target of positioning average cost in the bottom half of the industry cost curve.

Low Leverage Mitigates High Capex: Fitch expects NST's capex to remain high in FY26 to FY28 due to major growth projects. NST has indicated capex of AUD845 million-920 million in FY26 for the KCGM mill expansion and the readiness infrastructure for the project. It expects the project to be completed in FY27. NST's Hemi mine development is moving towards a final investment decision, with capex likely to be comparable with KCGM's and commencement targeted for FY27, subject to receiving final permits.

We estimate that the construction of Hemi, which has 6 million oz in reserves, will increase NST's EBITDA leverage to 1.1x in FY28 (FY25: 0.4x) under Fitch's price deck assumptions. However, leverage will remain well below the 3.3x level at which Fitch would consider negative rating action.

Improving Operational Scale: NST is carrying out its major growth project, the KCGM mill expansion, alongside other mine developments. The KCGM project will increase mill capacity from 13 mtpa to 23 mtpa in FY27 before ramping up to 27 mtpa by FY29. Fitch expects the KCGM development to allow the company to increase gold output to 2.0 million oz when operating at full capacity in FY28-FY30, while the development of Hemi may lift it further to 2.5 million oz a year, cementing its position among the world's top-five gold miners.

Growing Reserves Drive Growth: NST is making progress towards its medium-term production target of 2 million oz per year by focusing on operational efficiencies. This is supported by rising reserves at existing mines, which totalled 22.3 million oz at end-March 2025. This shows potential for life-of-mine extension even after the KCGM project is completed. The potential rise in reserves creates a solid base for further organic production growth while maintaining mine life at, or better than, that of investment-grade peers.

Favourable Mining Jurisdictions: NST's mines are in low-risk Tier 1 mining jurisdictions - Australia and the US. This limits NST's operational risk compared with peers that have larger exposure to higher-risk countries. We believe this mitigates cash repatriation risk and minimises operational exposure to unstable mining regulations that are often present in developing countries.

Peer Analysis

NST had output of 1.6 million oz in FY25 and targeted production of 1.7 million-1.85 million oz in FY26. This is below investment-grade peers Kinross Gold Corporation's (BBB/Stable) estimate of 2.0 million oz (+/-5%) in 2025 and AngloGold Ashanti plc's (BBB-/Stable) 2.9 million-3.2 million oz in 2025. NST's progress on the KCGM expansion and development of the Hemi mine will put it in a better position among its peers. The planned increase in production will not change the company's exposure to Tier 1 mines only, assuming lower operational risks than its peers.

NST forecasts FY26 AISC per oz will rise to USD1,495-1,755 (AUD2,300-2,700) from USD1,391 in FY25. The increase in costs is driven by cost inflation and large mine developments across its asset portfolio. The forecast range is higher than Agnico Eagle Mines Limited's (BBB+/Stable) 2025 AISC per oz estimate of USD1,250-1,300, or Kinross Gold's USD1,500 (+/-5%), and close to AngloGold Ashanti's guidance of USD1,491-1, 750.

NST's financial profile is strong compared with that of its investment-grade peers. The company's leverage decreased to 0.4x in FY25 (FY24: 0.6x), which is one of the lowest among peers.

Fitch’s Key Rating-Case Assumptions

Fitch's Key Assumptions Within the Rating Case for the Issuer:

-- Gold price of USD2,700/oz in 2026, USD2,000 in 2027 and USD1,800 thereafter;

-- Gold production of 1.8 million oz in FY26 and 2.0 million oz on average in FY27 - FY28;

-- Capex includes remaining AUD0.65 billion for KCGM mill expansion, and AUD0.66 million in related infrastructure.

-- Dividend payout ratio of 25% in FY26 and thereafter;

-- AISC of USD1,651/oz in FY25 before decreasing gradually to USD1,464/oz by FY28.

RATING SENSITIVITIES

Factors That Could, Individually or Collectively, Lead to Negative Rating Action/Downgrade

-- Sustained negative free cash flow;

-- EBITDA leverage increasing to above 3.3x on a sustained basis under Fitch's gold price assumptions.

Factors That Could, Individually or Collectively, Lead to Positive Rating Action/Upgrade

-- Increase in annual production volume to 2 million oz, while improving its cost position to the bottom half of the global gold cost curve and maintaining EBITDA leverage below 2.3x under Fitch's gold price assumptions.

Liquidity and Debt Structure

As of 30 September 2025, NST had AUD1.5 billion in available cash and bullion (FYE25: AUD1.7 billion), and AUD1.5 billion in undrawn committed credit facilities (FYE25: AUD1.5 billion) that mature in December 2027 and 2028. NST's long-term debt comprises USD600 million in senior unsecured bonds due April 2033.

Issuer Profile

NST is a major gold producer, with annual gold output of 1.6 million oz and operations across three production centres - Kalgoorlie and Yandal in Australia, and Pogo in the US. The company is also planning to develop the large-scale, low-cost Hemi gold project in the Pilbara region of Western Australia, which can add 0.6 million oz to group production when constructed.

REFERENCES FOR SUBSTANTIALLY MATERIAL SOURCE CITED AS KEY DRIVER OF RATING The principal sources of information used in the analysis are described in the Applicable Criteria. MACROECONOMIC ASSUMPTIONS AND SECTOR FORECASTS Click here to access Fitch's latest quarterly Global Corporates Sector Forecasts Monitor data file which aggregates key data points used in our credit analysis. Fitch's macroeconomic forecasts, commodity price assumptions, default rate forecasts, sector key performance indicators and sector-level forecasts are among the data items included. ESG Considerations

The highest level of ESG credit relevance is a score of '3', unless otherwise disclosed in this section. A score of '3' means ESG issues are credit-neutral or have only a minimal credit impact on the entity, either due to their nature or the way in which they are being managed by the entity. Fitch's ESG Relevance Scores are not inputs in the rating process; they are an observation on the relevance and materiality of ESG factors in the rating decision. For more information on Fitch's ESG Relevance Scores, visit https://www.fitchratings.com/topics/esg/products#esg-relevance-scores.

Eurozone Bond Yields Edge Rise; Spain, France to Complete Annual Issuance — Market Talk
data.description: Eurozone government bond yields edge higher after opening, tracking the direction of U.S. Treasury yields, though moves are contained. "Bunds enter calmer waters while eurozone government bond spreads test new lows," says Commerzank Research's Hauke Siemssen in a note. Spain and France will conduct their last government bond auctions of the year, while Austria will publish its borrowing plans for 2026 later in the day. The 10-year Bund yield edges up 0.4 basis points to 2.753%, while the 10-year French OAT yield is up 0.7 basis points at 3.500%, according to Tradeweb. (emese.bartha@wsj.com)

data.title: Japan's Nikkei ends at 3-week high as robot makers surge on physical AI bets
data.description:
By Junko Fujita

Japan's Nikkei share average closed at a three-week high on Thursday, extending its rally for a third session, as robot makers led gains on bets that physical AI will fuel growth.

The Nikkei climbed 2.33% to 51,028.42, its highest close since November 13. The broader Topix closed at a record high, rising 1.92% to 3,398.21.

Robot maker Fanuc jumped 12.98% to top the Nikkei gainers, extending its 18.4% surge this week after announcing a partnership with U.S. chip giant Nvidia to develop industrial robots powered by "physical AI", which integrates artificial intelligence with robotic hardware.

"The market focus has shifted to robotics-related shares from chip stocks. This means investors keep looking for new themes," said Kazuaki Shimada, chief strategist at IwaiCosmo Securities.

Fanuc's peer Yaskawa Electric jumped 11.37%. Earlier this week, Yaskawa also announced a tie-up in physical AI with SoftBank Group .

SoftBank Group jumped 9.18%.

Nabtesco , another robot maker, jumped 11.28%.

Chip-testing equipment maker Advantest slipped 0.77%.

"The market is not rising in a broad-based rally these days, but if investors sell some shares, they buy others, which means the money is rotating," Shimada said.

Banks rebounded from the previous session's declines, with Mitsubishi UFJ Financial Group and Sumitomo Mitsui Financial Group up 1.82% and 1.72%, respectively.

Drug maker Sumitomo Pharma fell 4.73% to become the biggest percentage loser on the Nikkei.

Of the more than 1,600 stocks trading on the Tokyo Stock Exchange's (TSE) prime market, 79% rose, 17% fell, and 3% traded flat.
